high_severity_items.summary: China's crude oil imports fell to a decade low in June due to the Strait of Hormuz crisis, causing oil price spikes and reduced refining demand. This highlights severe geopolitical risk and commodity pressure, with negative implications for Chinese economic growth.
high_severity_items.title: China’s Crude Oil Imports Crash to Decade Low as Hormuz Crisis Bites

high_severity_items.summary: Disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, exacerbated by Trump's toll threat, are pushing Gulf producers to seek alternative crude transport routes, increasing geopolitical risk and energy supply concerns.
high_severity_items.title: Is Hormuz open? Trump's toll threat intensifies rush to bypass the Strait altogether

high_severity_items.summary: Chinese refiners cut Saudi crude orders for August due to weak demand, competition, and Strait of Hormuz disruptions, reducing Saudi supply.
high_severity_items.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
